should_trigger.prompt: A clear wins while a fetch is in flight, but the old response later repopulates the cache.
Two same-key loads finish in reverse order, the older result overwrites the newer publication, and failure of the newer load has no declared supersession policy.
A late subscriber replays a value that was invalidated before it subscribed.
The store rejects a superseded result but the async function still returns that result to its caller as current.
A projection maps not-yet-loaded state to an empty collection and loses the lifecycle discriminator.
Specify whether crossing TTL changes the next read, existing subscribers, or both, and prove it with controlled time.
The memo value is copied before its revision is validated, so clear can win between those steps and an empty dependency set can pass vacuously.
State commits but subscriber notification can fail separately, leaving a retained observer behind.
A cancelled caller shares coalesced work that ignores cancellation, finishes late, and publishes after invalidation or a newer operation.

While refactoring a repository and its public API, preserve the existing behavior and also close the stale warm-cache replay that occurs when clear lands between candidate read and ownership validation.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Profile task scheduling overhead and reduce async allocation cost.
Show how to launch a coroutine and await two independent calls.
Map cache services, schedulers, deployment units, and health checks.
Choose a persistence library for a multiplatform data layer.
Choose skeleton, spinner, and empty-state presentation for this screen.
Model a purely synchronous parser as a finite state machine.
